perturbation.original_text: BONDS In witness whereof, and accepting the General Conditions and the Specific Conditions this Contract is subscribed in two (2) duplicates of the same content in the city of Bogotá on the thirtieth (30th) day of the month of January, 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: In witness whereof, and accepting the General Conditions and the Specific Conditions this Contract is subscribed in two (2) duplicates of the same content in the city of Bogotá on the thirtieth (30th) day of the month of January, 2012. BONDS
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'BONDS' provision to the end of the paragraph, rather than having it clearly at the beginning, obscures the requirement for bonds, making it less prominent and potentially overlooked.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1618 of the Colombian Civil Code requires that the intent of the contracting parties be clearly apparent. By burying a critical term like 'BONDS' at the end of a sentence about contract execution, the intent to require a bond is made less clear and prominent, potentially contradicting the spirit of this law.
perturbation.location: First page, paragraph before 'Characteristics Lower Limit'
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ﻿ No disponible - Gestor Normativo - Función Pública × Esta página no está disponible, la norma puede que ya no este disponible o se ha utilizado un enlace erróneo Intente nuevamente con la búsqueda de la avanzada de normas
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving 'BONDS' to the end of the paragraph obscures the requirement, making it less prominent and potentially overlooked. The law explanation suggests that this violates Article 1618 of the Colombian Civil Code, which requires the intent of the contracting parties to be clearly apparent. The structural flaw definition states that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The original text clearly places 'BONDS' at the start, emphasizing the requirement. The changed text buries 'BONDS', making it less discoverable. Given that the original placement was clear and the changed placement obscures it, the change constitutes a structural flaw leading to a legal contradiction. The lack of available scraped snippets doesn't negate the effect of obscuring the provision.

perturbation.original_text: 7.10 The amounts deposited by the SENDER in any of the bank accounts of ECOPETROL must come from the accounts owned by the SENDER, who by means of written communication before the subscription of the Contract will certify the origin of funds. This in accordance with the Policy for the Prevention and Control of Asset Laundering of ECOPETROL.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.10 The amounts deposited by the SENDER in any of the bank accounts of ECOPETROL must come from the accounts owned by the SENDER. This in accordance with the Policy for the Prevention and Control of Asset Laundering of ECOPETROL, who by means of written communication before the subscription of the Contract will certify the origin of funds.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the phrase about the SENDER providing written communication to *after* the phrase about the Asset Laundering Policy, it creates ambiguity about whose responsibility it is to certify the origin of funds. This obscures the SENDER's direct obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Law 526 of 1999 in Colombia addresses money laundering and requires clear identification of the source of funds.  By creating ambiguity about who is responsible for certifying the origin of funds, the altered text weakens the clarity and directness of the obligation to comply with anti-money laundering policies, potentially contradicting the intent of this law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ﻿ No disponible - Gestor Normativo - Función Pública × Esta página no está disponible, la norma puede que ya no este disponible o se ha utilizado un enlace erróneo Intente nuevamente con la búsqueda de la avanzada de normas
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for certifying the origin of funds, potentially weakening the clarity and directness of the obligation to comply with anti-money laundering policies. Law 526 of 1999 addresses money laundering and requires clear identification of the source of funds. The original text clearly places this responsibility on the SENDER, whereas the revised text blurs this line. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is unavailable, the Law Explanation and the Perturbation Explanation strongly suggest that the change undermines the intention of anti-money laundering laws. By obscuring the SENDER's direct obligation, the modified text creates a structural flaw that could lead to non-compliance and opens the legal obligation to dispute. Based on the Perturbation Definitions, this is a Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction.
